hook: RT and South China Morning Post — opposite political universes — both said the same thing about Israeli buffer zone in southern Lebanon. Nobody else did.

tweets.text: 14 buckets, 20 articles, 4 distinct frames in today's coverage.
Frame: SECURITY_DEFENSE (7 buckets). "The IDF's Golani Brigade announced on Tuesday that it recently advanced all the way to the Litani River, around 10 kilometers from the Isra…" — Jerusalem Post

Frame: MORALITY (5 buckets). "Israeli soldiers jailed for desecrating Virgin Mary statue
Two Israeli soldiers were sentenced to several weeks in military prison for dese…" — RT

Frame: QUALITY_OF_LIFE (4 buckets). "Lebanese in south refuse to flee again despite escalating Israeli strikes

Obaida Hitto reports from southern Lebanon, where residents say…" — Al Jazeera English

Frame: POLICY_PRESCRIPTION (2 buckets). "Netanyahu has pledged to ‘finish the job’ against Hezbollah. It’s a promise he can’t deliver on

The Israeli prime minister is using the ‘G…" — The Conversation

The paradox: Russian state media (RT) and Hong Kong's South China Morning Post ran near-identical coverage of the Virgin Mary desecration (pairwise similarity 0.835), both treating Israeli military conduct as a shared Christian-heritage moral offence rather than as a geopolitica…

Silence as data: `usa` didn't cover this — they covered US coverage of Lebanon is absent from this corpus; US press appears to have subordinated Lebanon buffer-zone reporting to Hormuz ceasefire and Iran nuclear stories on the same day. instead.

**Jerusalem Post** (israel): Only outlet to specify that the Golani Brigade used robots for portions of its Litani River advance, and to give a three-day casualty count (15 Hezbollah fighters killed).
